ocr.text: The analysis of malignancy types reveals significant data across various categories. In Hodgkin lymphoma, there are 986 total
cases, with 238 (24.1%) being pediatric, 710 (72.0%) classified as RCC, and 38 (3.9%) from the community. Thyroid cancer
accounts for 677 cases, with 34 (5.1%) pediatric cases, 227 (33.5%) categorized as RCC, and 415 (61.4%) from the community.
Adult n (%)

Bone/soft tissue sarcomas  545  152 (27.9)  273 (50.1)  120 (22.0)

Bone/soft tissue sarcomas comprise 545 cases, showing 152 (27.9%) pediatric cases, 273 (50.1%) as RCC, and 120 (22.0%)
from the community. Testicular cancer has 507 cases, with 30 (6.0%) pediatric cases, a notable 422 (83.3%) as RCC, and 54
(10.7%) from the community. Leukemia presents with 483 total cases, including 184 (38.0%) pediatric cases, 255 (52.7%) as
RCC, and 45 (9.3%) from the community. Brain tumors total 482 cases, with 172 (25.7%) pediatric cases, 221 (45.8%) as RCC,
and 89 (18.5%) from the community. Non-Hodgkin lymphoma has 421 cases, with 109 (25.8%) pediatric cases, 212 (50.4%) as
RCC, and 100 (23.8%) from the community. Other malignancies amount to 1248 cases, with 241 (19.3%) pediatric cases, 715
(57.3%) as RCC, and 292 (23.4%) from the community. The overall total of cases across all malignancy types is 5349, with
pediatric cases not specified in the total row.
